novel_features.name: Recipient email timeline
novel_features.description: Every email sent to a single recipient address, with delivery status and timestamps, ordered newest-first.
novel_features.rationale: Resend has no API filter by recipient — answering 'what did we send to alice@?' today requires scanning every email manually.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs to reconstruct what a single user has received — support tickets, GDPR exports, dispute investigations.

novel_features.name: Cross-event delivery trace
novel_features.description: Collapsed event chain for a single email: sent → delivered → opened → clicked → bounced, in one ordered table.
novel_features.rationale: The Resend API splits delivery state across /emails/{id} and /logs; reconstructing the chain requires two requests and client-side merging.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when debugging 'why didn't this arrive?' — one command shows whether it was sent, delivered, opened, or bounced and when.

novel_features.name: Audience inventory rollup
novel_features.description: Per-audience rollup: contact count, unsubscribed count, last-broadcast timestamp, recent open-rate.
novel_features.rationale: No aggregate endpoint exists; the dashboard shows one audience at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before planning a campaign — quickly see which audiences are healthy and recently engaged.

novel_features.name: Cross-audience contact lookup
novel_features.description: Find every audience, segment, and topic a contact (by email or name) belongs to in one query.
novel_features.rationale: Today this requires N API calls — one list per audience — and merging client-side.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when triaging unsubscribe requests, GDPR deletions, or 'why is bob getting this email?'.

novel_features.name: Broadcast performance dashboard
novel_features.description: Open / click / bounce rate across all broadcasts, sortable; not limited to a single broadcast or 30-day window.
novel_features.rationale: The Resend dashboard shows one broadcast at a time and caps the window at 30 days.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to compare campaign performance across the lifetime of the account.

novel_features.name: Domain health summary
novel_features.description: Verification + DKIM/SPF/DMARC status across every domain in one table, flags missing or unverified records.
novel_features.rationale: No aggregate endpoint exists; today this requires N GET /domains/{id} calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use on call-out / deliverability incidents to confirm all sending domains are fully verified.

novel_features.name: Deliverability summary
novel_features.description: Bounce rate, complaint rate, and suppression count over a rolling window (default 7d) computed from local event data.
novel_features.rationale: Resend's suppression/bounce lists aren't queryable in aggregate — direct answer to the documented deliverability blind spot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use weekly to monitor IP/domain reputation trends before they cause deliverability incidents.

novel_features.name: API-key rotation audit
novel_features.description: API keys sorted by age + last-used timestamp (joined from logs); flags stale keys older than N days.
novel_features.rationale: The /api-keys list endpoint does not include last_used_at at scale — joining requires log queries per key.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during quarterly security reviews — find unused keys that should be rotated or revoked.

narrative.headline: The full Resend API (100 endpoints across emails, broadcasts, audiences, contacts, domains, templates, webhooks) plus a local SQLite cache that powers cross-resource queries the dashboard and SDK cannot answer.
narrative.value_prop: The official Resend CLI is fast and one-shot per command; this CLI is the agent-native companion with --json --select --dry-run consistency, FTS5 search over sent emails, and rollups (audiences inventory, broadcasts performance, deliverability summary, contacts where, emails to <recipient>) that exist only because every Resend resource is mirrored locally.

quickstart.comment: Populate the local SQLite store with audiences, contacts, domains, broadcasts, and templates.
Cross-audience rollup the official CLI cannot produce.
FTS across emails, contacts, broadcasts, and templates.

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm RESEND_API_KEY is exported. Keys start with re_… and are visible at resend.com/api-keys.
Run `resend-pp-cli sync --full` — local-only commands read from the SQLite cache.
Resend documents Retry-After. Lower --rate-limit (default 0 = unbounded) or run sync in off-hours.
Resend caches DNS for ~24h; `domains verify <id>` triggers re-check, and `domains get <id>` shows current DKIM/SPF/DMARC state.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to answer cross-resource questions about a Resend account that the dashboard or SDK cannot answer in one call — what did we send to a specific recipient, which audiences is a contact in, how does broadcast performance trend over time, what's the 7-day bounce rate. Stick with `npx resend send` for one-shot transactional sends or with React Email for templates; this CLI is the agent-facing companion, not a replacement.

recipes.title: Reconstruct what a user has received
recipes.explanation: Use this for support tickets and GDPR exports — every email sent to one recipient, with delivery state.

recipes.title: Pre-campaign audience check
recipes.explanation: Run before scheduling a broadcast — surfaces audiences with high unsubscribe rates or stale engagement.

recipes.title: Weekly deliverability review
recipes.explanation: Trend snapshot for IP/domain reputation; pipe through `jq` to alert on threshold breaches.

recipes.title: Audit stale API keys
recipes.explanation: Find unused keys during quarterly security reviews.

recipes.title: Search every sent email for a phrase
